perturbation.original_text: 2.6 "Sponsorship Benefits" means the benefits Newegg will provide Allied, including the license granted in Section 6.1 and the fee stated in Schedule 3.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.6 "Sponsorship Benefits" means the benefits Newegg will provide Allied, excluding the license granted in Section 6.1 but including the fee stated in Schedule 3.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the definition of 'Sponsorship Benefits' in Section 2.6 to exclude the license granted in Section 6.1. This contradicts Section 4.2, which states Newegg shall provide to Allied the Sponsorship Benefits and Section 5.1 stating that Allied grants Newegg a license to use the Allied Marks. The perturbation creates uncertainty about whether the license is a core benefit of the sponsorship.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 Newegg shall provide to Allied the Sponsorship Benefits set out in Schedule 3, including paying the Sponsorship Fee as provided in that Schedule. Any value-added, goods and services, or similar tax or duty imposed by any government or tax authority on any Sponsorship Benefit shall be borne solely by Allied.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Sponsorship Benefits' to *exclude* the license granted in Section 6.1, which directly contradicts Section 4.2. Section 4.2 states Newegg shall provide Allied with the Sponsorship Benefits. Since the original definition of Sponsorship Benefits *included* the license, Section 4.2 is relying on that benefit to be provided. Now that the changed text excludes the license from the definition of Sponsorship benefits, a clear contradiction is established, because Section 4.2 mandates the provision of a benefit that Section 2.6 explicitly excludes.

perturbation.original_text: 14.1 Failure by Allied to perform and comply with any of its obligations in sections 5, 6, 7, 9, 11, 12 and 13 of this Agreement shall be deemed a material breach of this Agreement and Newegg shall have the right to terminate this Agreement immediately if Allied fails to cure the breach within fifteen (15) days following Newegg's written notice of the breach.
perturbation.changed_text: 14.1 Failure by Allied to perform and comply with any of its obligations in sections 5, 6, 7, 9, 11, 12 and 13 of this Agreement shall be deemed a material breach of this Agreement and Newegg shall have the right to terminate this Agreement immediately if Allied fails to cure the breach within thirty (30) days following Newegg's written notice of the breach.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the cure period for a material breach from fifteen (15) days to thirty (30) days in Section 14.1. This contradicts Section 14.3, which states that a default or breach must be cured within fifteen (15) days of the receipt of the notice. The perturbation creates uncertainty about the actual cure period, potentially leading to disputes over termination rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14.3 Except as provided in (i) Section 5.2 for immediate termination subject to cure provisions in Section 14.1 , (ii) Section 14.1 for termination following notice, and (iii) Section 14.5 for immediate termination without notice, if either Party defaults in the performance, or breaches any provision, of this Agreement, then the non- defaulting Party may give written notice to the defaulting Party requiring the default or breach to be cured, and if the default or breach is not cured within fifteen (15) days of the receipt of the notice, this Agreement shall, without prejudice to any accrued right, automatically terminate at the end of the fifteen (15) day period.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 14.1 modifies the cure period for a material breach from fifteen (15) days to thirty (30) days. This directly contradicts Section 14.3, which explicitly states that a default or breach must be cured within fifteen (15) days of the receipt of the notice. This introduces a clear inconsistency regarding the applicable cure period, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 15.10 Governing Law and Jurisdiction. Without reference to choice or conflict of law principles, this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, USA. The Parties unconditionally submit to exclusive jurisdiction of and accept as the exclusive venue for any legal proceeding involving this Agreement the state and federal courts located in the County of Los Angeles, California. Before any Party (the "Complaining Party") may bring any legal proceeding against the other (the "Non Complaining Party"), the Complaining Party shall first make a reasonable and good faith attempt to resolve all disputes privately by notifying and providing to the Non Complaining Party of the Complaining Party's complaints, reasons and supporting evidence for the complaints, and the reasonable steps Complaining Party would like the Non Complaining Party to take in order to address the complaints. If for any reason the Non-Complaining Party disagrees with either the complaint or the steps suggested to address the complaints, the Parties shall discuss and work on an amicable solution for at least thirty (30) days before the Complaining Party may bring any legal proceeding to resolve the complaints. Any dispute, claim or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the breach, termination, enforcement, interpretation, or validity thereof, including the determination of the scope and applicability of this agreement to arbitrate, shall be determined by arbitration in Los Angeles County, California, by an arbitrator of JAMS, in accordance with its arbitration rules and procedures then in effect. Judgment on the arbitrator's award may be entered in any court having jurisdiction. The prevailing Party in any dispute involving this Agreement shall be entitled to recover from the other Party its costs, expenses, and reasonable attorneys' fees (including any fees for expert witnesses, paralegals, or other legal service providers). This Section 15.10 shall not preclude or place any condition on any Party from seeking injunctive relief from a court of appropriate jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: 15.10 Governing Law and Jurisdiction. Without reference to choice or conflict of law principles, this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, USA. The Parties unconditionally submit to exclusive jurisdiction of and accept as the exclusive venue for any legal proceeding involving this Agreement the state and federal courts located in the County of Los Angeles, California. Before any Party (the "Complaining Party") may bring any legal proceeding against the other (the "Non Complaining Party"), the Complaining Party shall first make a reasonable and good faith attempt to resolve all disputes privately by notifying and providing to the Non Complaining Party of the Complaining Party's complaints, reasons and supporting evidence for the complaints, and the reasonable steps Complaining Party would like the Non Complaining Party to take in order to address the complaints. If for any reason the Non-Complaining Party disagrees with either the complaint or the steps suggested to address the complaints, the Parties shall discuss and work on an amicable solution for at least sixty (60) days before the Complaining Party may bring any legal proceeding to resolve the complaints. Any dispute, claim or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the breach, termination, enforcement, interpretation, or validity thereof, including the determination of the scope and applicability of this agreement to arbitrate, shall be determined by arbitration in Los Angeles County, California, by an arbitrator of JAMS, in accordance with its arbitration rules and procedures then in effect. Judgment on the arbitrator's award may be entered in any court having jurisdiction. The prevailing Party in any dispute involving this Agreement shall be entitled to recover from the other Party its costs, expenses, and reasonable attorneys' fees (including any fees for expert witnesses, paralegals, or other legal service providers). This Section 15.10 shall not preclude or place any condition on any Party from seeking injunctive relief from a court of appropriate jurisdiction. However, pursuing injunctive relief will be conditioned upon the parties first undergoing mediation for a period of thirty (30) days.
perturbation.explanation: The change modifies the initial dispute resolution process in Section 15.10. The original text requires parties to attempt to resolve disputes amicably for at least thirty (30) days before initiating legal proceedings. The changed text extends this period to sixty (60) days. Additionally, it adds a condition that seeking injunctive relief is contingent upon undergoing mediation for thirty (30) days. This introduces an in-text contradiction because while the original text allows for seeking injunctive relief without any preconditions, the modified text imposes a mediation requirement. This creates uncertainty as to when and how injunctive relief can be pursued.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15.10 Governing Law and Jurisdiction. Without reference to choice or conflict of law principles, this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, USA. The Parties unconditionally submit to exclusive jurisdiction of and accept as the exclusive venue for any legal proceeding involving this Agreement the state and federal courts located in the County of Los Angeles, California. Before any Party (the "Complaining Party") may bring any legal proceeding against the other (the "Non Complaining Party"), the Complaining Party shall first make a reasonable and good faith attempt to resolve all disputes privately by notifying and providing to the Non Complaining Party of the Complaining Party's complaints, reasons and supporting evidence for the complaints, and the reasonable steps Complaining Party would like the Non Complaining Party to take in order to address the complaints. If for any reason the Non-Complaining Party disagrees with either the complaint or the steps suggested to address the complaints, the Parties shall discuss and work on an amicable solution for at least sixty (60) days before the Complaining Party may bring any legal proceeding to resolve the complaints. Any dispute, claim or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the breach, termination, enforcement, interpretation, or validity thereof, including the determination of the scope and applicability of this agreement to arbitrate, shall be determined by arbitration in Los Angeles County, California, by an arbitrator of JAMS, in accordance with its arbitration rules and procedures then in effect. Judgment on the arbitrator's award may be entered in any court having jurisdiction. The prevailing Party in any dispute involving this Agreement shall be entitled to recover from the other Party its costs, expenses, and reasonable attorneys' fees (including any fees for expert witnesses, paralegals, or other legal service providers). This Section 15.10 shall not preclude or place any condition on any Party from seeking injunctive relief from a court of appropriate jurisdiction. However, pursuing injunctive relief will be conditioned upon the parties first undergoing mediation for a period of thirty (30) days.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 15.10 states that 'This Section 15.10 shall not preclude or place any condition on any Party from seeking injunctive relief from a court of appropriate jurisdiction.' The changed text modifies this by adding 'However, pursuing injunctive relief will be conditioned upon the parties first undergoing mediation for a period of thirty (30) days.' This directly contradicts the original clause by placing a condition (30-day mediation) on seeking injunctive relief, where previously no such condition existed. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction is present.
